title: Clay tries yet again to craft a noise ordinance for Micron construction
summary: The Clay Town Board is attempting a third passage of a noise ordinance that would permit Micron Technology to increase construction noise levels. This reflects ongoing negotiations between local government and a major economic development actor regarding land-use and construction standards.
department_refs: Town of Clay Planning and Zoning

political_signal.description: Repeated attempts to pass ordinance suggest Town prioritization of Micron project facilitation, though multiple failures indicate potential constituent opposition or technical/legal barriers.

processing_notes: Article source is syracuse.com but subject entity (Micron) is located in Town of Clay, not City of Syracuse—verify jurisdictional relevance to CIO office
Third attempt suggests legislative friction; underlying constituent or operational concerns not detailed in headline
No dollar amounts, timeline, or specific noise threshold metrics provided in extracted content
